mechanism: Participants are presented with binary-choice trials involving hidden targets (images, words). The participant makes a choice before the computer deterministically or randomly selects which option was the target, creating a temporal inversion of standard stimulus-response timing. Statistical analysis uses t-tests and binomial tests on hit rates to assess whether choice accuracy exceeds chance level (50%) by aggregate and individual-difference measures.
distribution: outcome: binomial hits expected at 0.5 under null

mechanism: Participants rate approachability (5-point Likert) on visual stimuli (faces, bodies, or composite images), responding to emotional expressions. Analysis uses repeated-measures ANOVA with emotion and visual-component as factors, followed by planned comparisons. Effect sizes are d or t-based. No explicit computational mechanism for generation; purely judgment aggregation.
distribution: outcome: rating aggregates analyzed via parametric ANOVA

mechanism: Participants recall or infer social relationships based on memory and word associations. Bathing frequency, duration, and water temperature are observed as correlates of chronic loneliness; physical temperature primes (warm/cold packs) manipulate state affect. Effects tested using Pearson correlation, ANOVA, and t-tests. No computational core; associative and self-regulatory.
distribution: outcome: continuous self-report measures analyzed via parametric tests

mechanism: Participants rate gender-ambiguous stimuli (names, numbers, babies' faces) on 7-point Likert scales. Odd vs. even numerical cues are paired with targets to test implicit gender associations. Repeated-measures ANOVA and t-tests compare ratings within-subject. No computational mechanism; association and misattribution-based judgment.
distribution: outcome: ratings analyzed via parametric ANOVA; null is scale midpoint

mechanism: Participants process word lists constructed from free association norms (progressive: chain of neighbors; stagnant: all associates of one target). After each block, affect is measured on PANAS (20-item scale). Repeated-measures ANOVA tests Block × Valence interaction. Words selected via forward-association strength thresholds from norm database.
distribution: outcome: ordinal scales (PANAS) aggregated and analyzed parametrically

mechanism: Participants view hierarchical and non-hierarchical stimuli (faces, diagrams, relationship sets) and measure processing speed (reaction time, learning time), memory retention, and liking (Likert scales). Effects tested via repeated-measures ANOVA, t-tests, and regression. No explicit computational generation; observation and rating aggregation.
distribution: outcome: continuous measures (RT, liking) analyzed via parametric tests
